NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. Hear my voice, O God, in my meditation; Preserve my life from fear of the enemy.
NKJV.2: Hide me from the secret plots of the wicked, From the rebellion of the workers of iniquity,
NKJV.3: Who sharpen their tongue like a sword, And bend their bows to shoot their arrows—bitter words,
NKJV.4: That they may shoot in secret at the blameless; Suddenly they shoot at him and do not fear.
NKJV.5: They encourage themselves in an evil matter; They talk of laying snares secretly; They say, “Who will see them?”
NKJV.6: They devise iniquities: “We have perfected a shrewd scheme.” Both the inward thought and the heart of man are deep.
NKJV.7: But God shall shoot at them with an arrow; Suddenly they shall be wounded.
NKJV.8: So He will make them stumble over their own tongue;  All who see them shall flee away.
NKJV.9: All men shall fear, And shall declare the work of God; For they shall wisely consider His doing.
NKJV.10: The righteous shall be glad in the LORD, and trust in Him. And all the upright in heart shall glory.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. Hear<S>8085</S> my voice<S>6963</S>, O God<S>430</S>, in my prayer<S>7879</S>: preserve<S>5341</S> my life<S>2416</S> from fear<S>6343</S> of the enemy<S>341</S>.
KJV.2: Hide<S>5641</S> me from the secret counsel<S>5475</S> of the wicked<S>7489</S>; from the insurrection<S>7285</S> of the workers<S>6466</S> of iniquity<S>205</S>:
KJV.3: Who whet<S>8150</S> their tongue<S>3956</S> like a sword<S>2719</S>, and bend<S>1869</S> their bows to shoot their arrows<S>2671</S>, even bitter<S>4751</S> words<S>1697</S>:
KJV.4: That they may shoot<S>3384</S> in secret<S>4565</S> at the perfect<S>8535</S>: suddenly<S>6597</S> do they shoot<S>3384</S> at him, and fear<S>3372</S> not.
KJV.5: They encourage<S>2388</S> themselves in an evil<S>7451</S> matter<S>1697</S>: they commune<S>5608</S> of laying<S>2934</S> snares<S>4170</S> privily<S>2934</S>; they say<S>559</S>, Who shall see<S>7200</S> them? <sup>matter: or, speech</sup> <sup>of: Heb. to hide his snares</sup>
KJV.6: They search out<S>2664</S> iniquities<S>5766</S>; they accomplish<S>8552</S> a diligent<S>2664</S> search<S>2665</S>: both the inward<S>7130</S> thought of every one<S>376</S> of them, and the heart<S>3820</S>, is deep<S>6013</S>. <sup>they: or, we are consumed by that which they have throughly searched</sup> <sup>a diligent: Heb. a search searched</sup>
KJV.7: But God<S>430</S> shall shoot<S>3384</S> at them with an arrow<S>2671</S>; suddenly<S>6597</S> shall they be wounded<S>4347</S>. <sup>shall they: Heb. their wound shall be</sup>
KJV.8: So they shall make their own tongue<S>3956</S> to fall<S>3782</S> upon themselves: all that see<S>7200</S> them shall flee away<S>5074</S>.
KJV.9: And all men<S>120</S> shall fear<S>3372</S>, and shall declare<S>5046</S> the work<S>6467</S> of God<S>430</S>; for they shall wisely consider<S>7919</S> of his doing<S>4639</S>.
KJV.10: The righteous<S>6662</S> shall be glad<S>8055</S> in the LORD<S>3068</S>, and shall trust<S>2620</S> in him; and all the upright<S>3477</S> in heart<S>3820</S> shall glory<S>1984</S>.

ESV.1: Hear my voice, O God, in my complaint; preserve my life from dread of the enemy.
ESV.2: Hide me from the secret plots of the wicked, from the throng of evildoers,
ESV.3: who whet their tongues like swords, who aim bitter words like arrows,
ESV.4: shooting from ambush at the blameless, shooting at him suddenly and without fear.
ESV.5: They hold fast to their evil purpose; they talk of laying snares secretly, thinking, “Who can see them?”
ESV.6: They search out injustice, saying, “We have accomplished a diligent search.” For the inward mind and heart of a man are deep.
ESV.7: But God shoots his arrow at them; they are wounded suddenly.
ESV.8: They are brought to ruin, with their own tongues turned against them; all who see them will wag their heads.
ESV.9: Then all mankind fears; they tell what God has brought about and ponder what he has done.
ESV.10: Let the righteous one rejoice in the LORD and take refuge in him! Let all the upright in heart exult!

NASB.1: For the choir director. A Psalm of David. Hear my voice, O God, in my complaint; Preserve my life from dread of the enemy.
NASB.2: Hide me from the secret counsel of evildoers, From the tumult of those who do iniquity,
NASB.3: Who have sharpened their tongue like a sword. They aimed bitter speech [as] their arrow,
NASB.4: To shoot from concealment at the blameless; Suddenly they shoot at him, and do not fear.
NASB.5: They hold fast to themselves an evil purpose; They talk of laying snares secretly; They say, 'Who can see them?'
NASB.6: They devise injustices, [saying], 'We are ready with a well-conceived plot'; For the inward thought and the heart of a man are deep.
NASB.7: But God will shoot at them with an arrow; Suddenly they will be wounded.
NASB.8: So they will make him stumble; Their own tongue is against them; All who see them will shake the head.
NASB.9: Then all men will fear, And they will declare the work of God, And will consider what He has done.
NASB.10: The righteous man will be glad in the LORD and will take refuge in Him; And all the upright in heart will glory.

NLT.1:
O God, listen to my complaint.
Protect my life from my enemies’ threats.
NLT.2:
Hide me from the plots of this evil mob,
from this gang of wrongdoers.
NLT.3:
They sharpen their tongues like swords
and aim their bitter words like arrows.
NLT.4:
They shoot from ambush at the innocent,
attacking suddenly and fearlessly.
NLT.5:
They encourage each other to do evil
and plan how to set their traps in secret.
“Who will ever notice?” they ask.
NLT.6:
As they plot their crimes, they say,
“We have devised the perfect plan!”
Yes, the human heart and mind are cunning.
NLT.7:
But God himself will shoot them with his arrows,
suddenly striking them down.
NLT.8:
Their own tongues will ruin them,
and all who see them will shake their heads in scorn.
NLT.9:
Then everyone will be afraid;
they will proclaim the mighty acts of God
and realize all the amazing things he does.
NLT.10:
The godly will rejoice in the LORD
and find shelter in him.
And those who do what is right
will praise him.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician. A Psalm by David. Hear my voice, God, in my complaint. Preserve my life from fear of the enemy.
WEB.2: Hide me from the conspiracy of the wicked, from the noisy crowd of the ones doing evil;
WEB.3: who sharpen their tongue like a sword, and aim their arrows, deadly words,
WEB.4: to shoot innocent men from ambushes. They shoot at him suddenly and fearlessly.
WEB.5: They encourage themselves in evil plans. They talk about laying snares secretly. They say, “Who will see them?”
WEB.6: They plot injustice, saying, “We have made a perfect plan!” Surely man’s mind and heart are cunning.
WEB.7: But God will shoot at them. They will be suddenly struck down with an arrow.
WEB.8: Their own tongues shall ruin them. All who see them will shake their heads.
WEB.9: All mankind shall be afraid. They shall declare the work of God, and shall wisely ponder what he has done.
WEB.10: The righteous shall be glad in Yahweh, and shall take refuge in him. All the upright in heart shall praise him!

CSB17.1:

Protect my life from the terror of the enemy. ⓛ

Hide me from the scheming of wicked people, ⓜ
from the mob of evildoers, ⓝ

who sharpen their tongues like swords
and aim bitter words like arrows, ⓐ

shooting from concealed places at the blameless.

They adopt [1] an evil plan;
they talk about hiding traps and say,

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician. A Psalm of David. Hear my voice, O God, in my complaint;
Guard my life from the terror of the enemy.
AMP.2:
Hide me from the secret counsel and conspiracy of the ungodly,
From the scheming of those who do wrong,
AMP.3:
Who have sharpened their tongues like a sword.
They aim venomous words as arrows,
AMP.4:
To shoot from ambush at the blameless [one];
Suddenly they shoot at him, without fear.
AMP.5:
They encourage themselves in [their pursuit of] an evil agenda;
They talk of laying snares secretly;
They say, "Who will discover us?"
AMP.6:
They devise acts of injustice, saying,
"We are ready with a well-conceived plan."
For the inward thought and the heart of a man are deep (mysterious, unsearchable).
AMP.7:
But God will shoot them with an [unexpected] arrow;
Suddenly they will be wounded.
AMP.8:
So they will be caused to stumble;
Their own tongue is against them;
All who gaze at them will shake the head [in scorn].
AMP.9:
Then all men will fear [God's judgment];
They will declare the work of God,
And they will consider and wisely acknowledge what He has done.
AMP.10:
The righteous will rejoice in the LORD and take refuge in Him;
All the upright in heart will glory and offer praise.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Listen and help, O God.
I'm reduced to a whine
And a whimper, obsessed
with feelings of doomsday.
MSG.2:
Don't let them find me—
the conspirators out to get me,
MSG.3:
Using their tongues as weapons,
flinging poison words,
poison-tipped arrow-words.
MSG.4:
They shoot from ambush,
shoot without warning,
not caring who they hit.
MSG.5:
They keep fit doing calisthenics
of evil purpose,
They keep lists of the traps
they've secretly set.
They say to each other,
"No one can catch us,
MSG.6:
no one can detect our perfect crime."
The Detective detects the mystery
in the dark of the cellar heart.
MSG.7:
The God of the Arrow shoots!
They double up in pain,
MSG.8:
Fall flat on their faces
in full view of the grinning crowd.
MSG.9:
Everyone sees it. God's
work is the talk of the town.
MSG.10:
Be glad, good people! Fly to God!
Good-hearted people, make praise your habit.

NIV.1:
Psalm 64
For the director of music. A psalm of David.
Hear me, O God, as I voice my complaint;
protect my life from the threat of the enemy.
NIV.2:
Hide me from the conspiracy of the wicked,
from that noisy crowd of evildoers.
NIV.3:
They sharpen their tongues like swords
and aim their words like deadly arrows.
NIV.4:
They shoot from ambush at the innocent man;
they shoot at him suddenly, without fear.
NIV.5:
They encourage each other in evil plans,
they talk about hiding their snares;
they say, “Who will see them?”
NIV.6:
They plot injustice and say,
“We have devised a perfect plan!”
Surely the mind and heart of man are cunning.
NIV.7:
But God will shoot them with arrows;
suddenly they will be struck down.
NIV.8:
He will turn their own tongues against them
and bring them to ruin;
all who see them will shake their heads in scorn.
NIV.9:
All mankind will fear;
they will proclaim the works of God
and ponder what he has done.
NIV.10:
Let the righteous rejoice in the Lord
and take refuge in him;
let all the upright in heart praise him!

NRSVCE.1:
Hear my voice, O God, in my complaint;
preserve my life from the dread enemy.
NRSVCE.2:
Hide me from the secret plots of the wicked,
from the scheming of evildoers,
NRSVCE.3:
who whet their tongues like swords,
who aim bitter words like arrows,
NRSVCE.4:
shooting from ambush at the blameless;
they shoot suddenly and without fear.
NRSVCE.5:
They hold fast to their evil purpose;
they talk of laying snares secretly,
thinking, “Who can see us?
NRSVCE.6:
Who can search out our crimes?
We have thought out a cunningly conceived plot.”
For the human heart and mind are deep.
NRSVCE.7:
But God will shoot his arrow at them;
they will be wounded suddenly.
NRSVCE.8:
Because of their tongue he will bring them to ruin;
all who see them will shake with horror.
NRSVCE.9:
Then everyone will fear;
they will tell what God has brought about,
and ponder what he has done.
NRSVCE.10:
Let the righteous rejoice in the LORD
and take refuge in him.
Let all the upright in heart glory.

NET.1: Listen to me, O God, as I offer my lament! Protect my life from the enemy’s terrifying attacks.
NET.2: Hide me from the plots of evil men, from the crowd of evildoers.
NET.3: They sharpen their tongues like a sword; they aim their arrow, a slanderous charge,
NET.4: in order to shoot down the innocent in secluded places. They shoot at him suddenly and are unafraid of retaliation.
NET.5: They encourage one another to carry out their evil deed. They plan how to hide snares, and boast, “Who will see them?”
NET.6: They devise unjust schemes; they disguise a well-conceived plot. Man’s inner thoughts cannot be discovered.
NET.7: But God will shoot at them; suddenly they will be wounded by an arrow.
NET.8: Their slander will bring about their demise. All who see them will shudder,
NET.9: and all people will fear. They will proclaim what God has done, and reflect on his deeds.
NET.10: The godly will rejoice in the LORD and take shelter in him. All the morally upright will boast.
